text: Nice! reminds me of Tldraw’s drag handles: <https://twitter.com/tldraw/status/1464638953227788294?lang=en>

elements.text: Nice! reminds me of Tldraw’s drag handles:

attachments.video_html: <video autoplay loop muted poster="https://pbs.twimg.com/tweet_video_thumb/FFNwWOvWYAskQo8.jpg" width="720" height="406"><source type="video/mp4" src="https://video.twimg.com/tweet_video/FFNwWOvWYAskQo8.mp4"></video>
attachments.fallback: <https://twitter.com/tldraw|@tldraw>: Ever wonder what those three arrows are for? :bow_and_arrow: They'll appear if your selected shape has arrows connected to it, and you can use them to move the network of shapes "pointing to" or "pointing away from" your selection.
attachments.text: Ever wonder what those three arrows are for? :bow_and_arrow: They'll appear if your selected shape has arrows connected to it, and you can use them to move the network of shapes "pointing to" or "pointing away from" your selection.

text: Thanks, that is super cool. I'd like to add something like that. Right now you can do it through a keyboard shortcut (cmd-shift-arrow). But I'm trying to add more mouse affordances. I think with these abstract concepts it can be hard to come up with a good UI that is easy to discover though.

elements.text: Thanks, that is super cool. I'd like to add something like that. Right now you can do it through a keyboard shortcut (cmd-shift-arrow). But I'm trying to add more mouse affordances. I think with these abstract concepts it can be hard to come up with a good UI that is easy to discover though.
